title: TGHE: Template-based Graph Homomorphic Encryption for Privacy-Preserving GNN Inference in Edge-Cloud Systems
abstract: arXiv:2606.26664v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Existing homomorphic encryption (HE)-based GNN systems adopt a graph-centric paradigm that couples per-query cost to global graph size, limiting evaluations to at most ~20k nodes and making them incompatible with dynamic, large-scale financial graphs. We propose TGHE (Template-based Graph Homomorphic Encryption), an ego-centric framework that resolves this by exploiting a template phenomenon: local computation trees in transaction graphs converge into a small set of structural shapes. TGHE canonicalizes ego-graphs at the edge and packs structurally identical trees into shared CKKS ciphertexts for SIMD-parallel encrypted inference, with two long-tail optimizers (Approximate Template Fitting and Topology Collapse) ensuring full SIMD coverage. On DGraphFin (3.7M nodes, 4.3M edges), TGHE-Collapse achieves a 66.9x speedup over the sequential encrypted baseline with less than 0.002 AUC loss.

Title: TGHE: Template-based Graph Homomorphic Encryption for Privacy-Preserving GNN Inference in Edge-Cloud Systems
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26664v1 Announce Type: cross  Abstract: Existing homomorphic encryption (HE)-based GNN systems adopt a graph-centric paradigm that couples per-query cost to global graph size, limiting evaluations to at most ~20k nodes and making them incompatible with dynamic, large-scale financial graphs. We propose TGHE (Template-based Graph Homomorphic Encryption), an ego-centric framework that resolves this by exploiting a template phenomenon: local computation trees in transaction graphs converge into a small set of structural shapes. TGHE canonicalizes ego-graphs at the edge and packs structurally identical trees into shared CKKS ciphertexts for SIMD-parallel encrypted inference, with two long-tail optimizers (Approximate Template Fitting and Topology Collapse) ensuring full SIMD coverage. On DGraphFin (3.7M nodes, 4.3M edges), TGHE-Collapse achieves a 66.9x speedup over the sequential encrypted baseline with less than 0.002 AUC loss.
translated_title: TGHE：基于模板的图同态加密方法用于边缘-云系统中隐私保护的GNN推理
translated_abstract: 现有的基于同态加密（HE）的GNN系统采用图中心化范式，将每次查询的成本与全局图规模耦合，使得评估最多只能处理约2万个节点，并且无法兼容动态、大规模金融图。我们提出TGHE（基于模板的图同态加密），这是一种自我中心框架，通过利用模板现象来解决这一问题：交易图中的局部计算树会收敛为少量结构形状。TGHE在边缘端对自我图进行规范化处理，并将结构相同的树打包到共享的CKKS密文中，用于SIMD并行加密推理，同时配备两种长尾优化器（近似模板拟合与拓扑坍缩）以确保完全SIMD覆盖。在DGraphFin（370万个节点、430万条边）上，TGHE-Collapse相比顺序加密基线实现了66.9倍加速，且AUC损失低于0.002。
tldr: TGHE通过将动态金融图中的局部计算树模板化并打包为共享密文，实现了大规模图上的隐私保护GNN推理，在保持高精度的同时获得近67倍加速。
en_tdlr: TGHE achieves near 67x speedup for privacy-preserving GNN inference on large-scale dynamic financial graphs by templating and packing local computation trees into shared ciphertexts, with less than 0.002 AUC loss.
